reasoning: Hamas considers its armed wing (Al-Qassam Brigades) and its military capabilities as its core existential tool and political leverage. Historically, militant/terrorist organizations engaged in an existential, ideologically-driven conflict almost never voluntarily announce disarmament unless they are signing a comprehensive peace treaty that integrates them into a state apparatus—an outcome virtually impossible by the end of 2025 given the current Israeli government's strong opposition to an independent Palestinian state and any political role for Hamas. Furthermore, the resolution criteria strictly exclude statements contingent on future conditions (e.g., Hamas offering to disarm only if an independent Palestinian state is established). An outright, official announcement of unconditioned disarmament or surrender by Hamas, even partial, flies against its entire organizational history and ideology. Therefore, the likelihood of Hamas issuing an official policy of disarmament by December 2025 is extremely minimal.
